rulings.Case Title: HARRY HECKET et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cooper Industries, Llc F/K/A Cooper Industries' Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 18, 2009, line 11, Defendant Cooper Industries, LLC F/K/A Cooper Industries' Motion For Summary Judgment. Cooper Industries' motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff received sufficient notice of the hearing on the motion and did not file any substantive opposition. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HARRY HECKET et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant General Cable Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 18, 2009, line 11, Defendant General Cable Corporation's Motion For Summary Judgment. General Cable¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. General Cable failed to meet its burden of production and, even if it did, plaintiff has raised a triable issue of fact regarding his exposure to General Cable¿s asbestos-containing wires and cables. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion And Petition [Under Ccp 1285, Et Sq.] Of Defts To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 18, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANTS PACIFIC LAPAROSCOPY, JOHN RABKIN, ROBERT RABKIN, ROSSI, PA-C, MICHAEL Petition [Under CCP 1285, Et Sq.] To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUTUAL CORP et al VS. HAWK N LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs City And County Of San Francisco And People Of The State Of California'S Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 18, 2009, line 3, Plaintiffs City And County Of San Francisco And People Of The State Of California'S Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt. GRANT. The Ng defendants and the Lee defendants shall each pay $6,099 in attorneys' fees to the plaintiffs no later than July 31, 2009. Both injunctions are extended for another six months. Penalties of $25,000 against the Ng defendants and the Lee defendants are stayed on the condition that the defendants have performed all of their obligations under the injunctions on or before December 18, 2009. If the defendants are not in compliance with their obligations under the injunctions on or before December 18, 2009 the penalties of $25,000 will be imposed and the plaintiff may seek additional penalties for the time period beginning June 18, 2009. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WU'S CONSTRUCTION INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 2270 MISSION STREET,LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 18, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANTS 2270 MISSION STREET, LLC AND JUSTIN TIN DDS Motion To Set Aside Default. MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO PAY $1,760 (6 HOURS AT $295 PER HOUR) TO PLAINTIFF NO LATER THAN JULY 31, 2009 FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES REASONABLY EXPENDED BY PLAINTIFF. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN J. HECKTMAN et al VS. BING LOUIE LOUIE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Dismissal Of Cross-Complaint Of Tami Twarog)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 18, 2009, line 9, PLAINTIFFS BRIAN HECKTMAN, SANDRA DELRAHIM, XON BURRIS, JANET BURRIS, ELIZABETH BURRIS, JOY DIFRANZA, SARA MENUCK'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Dismissal Of Cross-Complaint Of Tami Twarog). Plaintiffs' motions for judgment on the pleadings denied. Liberally construed, the cross-complaints allege that cross-complainants are third-party beneficiaries of the 1914 indenture and thus they allege standing to seek the relief sought in the cross-complaints. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN J. HECKTMAN et al VS. BING LOUIE LOUIE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Dismissal Of Cross-Complaint Of Elizabeth A. Wright)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 18, 2009, line 9, PLAINTIFFS BRIAN HECKTMAN, SANDRA DELRAHIM, XON BURRIS, JANET BURRIS, ELIZABETH BURRIS, JOY DIFRANZA'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Dismissal Of Cross-Complaint Of Elizabeth A. Wright). Plaintiffs' motions for judgment on the pleadings denied. Liberally construed, the cross-complaints allege that cross-complainants are third-party beneficiaries of the 1914 indenture and thus they allege standing to seek the relief sought in the cross-complaints. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN BUTLER VS. BUSINESS FOR SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 18, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT BUSINESS FOR SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer to the portion of the third cause of action alleging age discrimination is sustained. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege a new fourth cause of action for age discrimination distinct from his third cause of action for race discrimination and to allege, if he is able to do so, that someone younger than him was treated more favorably. =(302/HEK)
